fields.description:
## Why

Today a plan-review criterion is inert unless it is in the hardcoded `CANONICAL_LLM` frozenset
(`plan_review/registry.py:70`) AND the packaged `criteria_routing.json`; `_routing_index()` /
`load_criteria()` are `@lru_cache`d with no `repo_root` key. So a project cannot add a criterion at
all. This story opens the vocabulary and adds the project overlay â€” the **dependency root + MVP
slice** for the whole epic (`3156`). It is NOT blocked on `b744` (extends plan-review directly).

## What

- `effective_criteria(repo_root)` = `CANONICAL_LLM` âˆª activated project ids; route it through
  **every** plan-review vocabulary site: `load_criteria`, `check_registry_coverage`, and the
  workflow Pass-1 batch vocab in `workflow_ops` (`plan_review_assemble_criteria`). The
  `include_<id>` output key must sanitize the `project.<name>` id (`.`â†’`_`) at BOTH the assemble
  and consume sites (keep the rule co-located).
- `.rebar/criteria_routing.json` overlay: gate-keyed (`{plan_review: {...}, activate: [...]}`),
  reusing the packaged routing schema; `project.<name>` namespace; an un-prefixed built-in id =
  re-tune/disable; a `project.`-id colliding with a built-in, or a duplicate, is **rejected at
  load** (located error). A criterion runs only if in `activate`.
- Cache isolation: keep `_routing_index()` cached for the packaged index; add
  `effective_routing(repo_root)` (uncached, or keyed by `(repo_root, overlay mtime)` â€” note mtime
  granularity, prefer content hash if flaky); extend `prompt_library._invalidate_caches()`.
- Routing validation: load-time (invalid routing = located error, not silent skip) + a CI
  drift/parity gate mirroring `reviewers/index.json`.

## Scope

- Files: `src/rebar/llm/plan_review/registry.py`, `src/rebar/llm/plan_review/workflow_ops.py`,
  `src/rebar/llm/prompt_library.py`, `src/rebar/llm/plan_review/criteria_routing.json` (schema doc).
- MVP end-to-end: activate ONE project LLM criterion from the overlay â†’ it runs in plan-review
  Pass-1 â†’ surfaces a finding. (Attestation invalidation is story `#4`; unify is story `#6`.)
- Out: code-review side (story `#6`, b744-blocked); DET consumer (story `#2`).

## Acceptance Criteria

- [ ] `effective_criteria(repo_root)` loads activated project criteria and is routed through
      `load_criteria`, `check_registry_coverage`, and the workflow Pass-1 batch vocab; an activated
      project LLM criterion fans out and runs in plan-review Pass-1.
- [ ] `.rebar/criteria_routing.json` overlay per schema: gate-keyed, `project.<name>` namespace,
      explicit `activate` list, load-time collision/rebind rejection (located error).
- [ ] `include_<id>` key sanitization (`.`â†’`_`) applied at both assemble + consume sites.
- [ ] Overlay merge is cache-isolated per repo (packaged index stays cached; overlay is not);
      a RED test proves no cross-repo routing leakage in the long-lived MCP server.
- [ ] Routing validation: invalid overlay routing is a located error + a CI drift/parity gate.
- [ ] An ADR documents the unified-registry + overlay merge/activation contract.
- [ ] MVP slice demonstrated end-to-end (activate one criterion â†’ runs â†’ surfaces a finding).

## Backward-compatibility, fan-out mechanism & cache strategy (remediation, run 1)

### Expand-contract / backward-compatibility (clears T4)
Every signature change is **additive** with a `repo_root=None` default that preserves
today's behaviour, so no existing caller breaks:
- `registry.load_criteria(repo_root=None)`, `registry.by_id(repo_root=None)`,
  `registry.check_registry_coverage(repo_root=None)`, `registry._descriptor_from_prompt(cid,
  *, repo_root=None, routing_index=None)`, and the NEW `effective_criteria(repo_root=None)` /
  `effective_routing(repo_root=None)` all default `repo_root=None`.
- `repo_root=None` resolves to `config.repo_root()` (the same root `get_prompt` already
  resolves `.rebar/prompts/` overrides against) â€” so an **overlay-absent** repo yields
  **exactly** today's packaged-only vocabulary/routing (no behaviour change, no churn).
- **Call sites updated** (enumerated): `orchestrator.route_criteria` (passes the ctx
  repo_root), `workflow_ops.plan_review_assemble_criteria` (uses `ctx.repo_root`),
  `attest.registry_version`/`plan_review.__init__` coverage (story #4 threads repo_root;
  this story keeps the no-arg default working), and the tests. Bare `load_criteria()` /
  `check_registry_coverage()` keep working unchanged.
- **Rollback:** delete `.rebar/criteria_routing.json` (overlay-absent â‡’ packaged behaviour),
  or revert the additive params (all default `None`).

### Dynamic Pass-1 fan-out mechanism (clears G6/T1/T3)
The batch step's `criteria:` list is STATIC YAML; a runtime-activated project id has no
static `when:` slot. Mechanism (additive, expand-contract): a new **optional
`batch.extra_criteria`** field on the v3 `batch` step â€” a single templated expression
resolving to a `[{prompt, when}]` list that the interpreter CONCATENATES with the static
`criteria` list before inclusion filtering. `plan_review_assemble_criteria` emits:
- `include_<sanitized_id>` booleans for activated project ids (`.`â†’`_`; e.g.
  `project.no_print` â†’ `include_project_no_print`), alongside the existing built-in
  `include_<ID>` booleans; and
- `project_criteria`: `[{"prompt": "plan-review-project.<name>", "when": "${{
  steps.assemble.outputs.include_project_<name> }}"}]`.
The gate YAML gains `extra_criteria: '${{ steps.assemble.outputs.project_criteria }}'` on
the finders batch. Built-ins are byte-identical (their static list is untouched); a repo
with no active project criterion emits an empty `project_criteria` â‡’ no change. The
`.`â†’`_` sanitization is co-located at BOTH the assemble site (produce `include_project_*`)
and the consume site (the `when` string in `project_criteria` references the same key); the
prompt id keeps the dotted `project.<name>` form (resolved verbatim by `get_prompt` as
`.rebar/prompts/plan-review-project.<name>.md`).

### Cache strategy â€” content-hash, not mtime (clears E2/G6/T8/T9)
`effective_routing`/`load_criteria` are memoized in repo-keyed module dicts keyed by
`(repo_root, sha256(overlay-bytes))` â€” a **content signature**, NOT mtime (mtime
granularity is coarse/flaky), so editing the overlay auto-invalidates the memo. The
packaged `_routing_index()` stays `@lru_cache`d (immutable per binary). Stale-entry
growth is bounded: `prompt_library._invalidate_caches()` clears both memos + the lru_caches
(the RED cross-repo-leak test proves repo A's routing never serves repo B).

### Sanitization inverse & missing-prompt fallback (clears T8)
The `.`â†’`_` map is one-directional at the workflow-key layer only; the criterion id and
prompt id retain the dotted form, so no inverse un-sanitization is needed (the
`project_criteria` entry carries the real dotted `prompt` id directly). An activated
project id whose prompt file is absent/malformed surfaces as a **located `RegistryError`**
from `load_criteria` (same as a missing built-in prompt) â€” fail-loud at load, never a
silent skip.

### ADR contents (clears E1/AC6)
`docs/adr/0012-project-supplied-criteria.md` documents: the `.rebar/criteria_routing.json`
overlay schema (gate-keyed, `project.<name>` namespace, `activate` list); the merge +
collision/rebind rules; `effective_criteria`/`effective_routing` as the vocabulary seam;
the content-hash cache-isolation contract; and the additive `batch.extra_criteria` fan-out.

### Scope clarification (clears E5/T8/G5)
MVP end-to-end exercises ONE activated project **LLM** criterion (`exec: 1-TURN`). Project
`exec: DET` criteria (the prompt-less descriptor branch) are story #2; the shared
`rebar.llm.criteria` unification is story #6. The CI drift/parity gate here is a
**validation** gate (the packaged `criteria_routing.json` stays schema-valid and in parity
with `CANONICAL_LLM` + the prompt files), not a generator, since the routing is
hand-authored judgement (thresholds/applies_at) with no derivation source.

## Acceptance Criteria (remediation additions)

- [ ] All new/changed registry signatures are additive with a `repo_root=None` default that
      preserves packaged-only behaviour (overlay-absent repo == today); rollback = delete the
      overlay.
- [ ] The dynamic Pass-1 fan-out uses an additive `batch.extra_criteria` templated list so an
      activated project criterion runs WITHOUT regenerating the static builtin fan-out.
